Title: Genome-wide identification and expression analysis of the coronatine-insensitive 1 (COI1) gene family in response to biotic and abiotic stresses in Saccharum

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: ShCOI1-4 is a ROC22-derived homolog of SsCOI1-4b with 99.50% amino acid identity and contains conserved F-box, Transp_inhibit, and AMN1 (LRR) domains. Compared with maize ZmCOI proteins, it retains only 4–5 of 16 reported JA-Ile/JAZ-binding residues, suggesting possible functional divergence among COI1 orthologs.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. ShCOI1-4 was cloned from sugarcane cultivar ROC22 as a homolog of SsCOI1-4b.
2. The encoded protein carries conserved F-box, Transp_inhibit, and AMN1 (LRR) domains characteristic of COI1 F-box proteins.
3. ShCOI1-4 transcripts are strongly induced by ABA and SA but are repressed by cold (4 °C) and PEG-induced drought stress.
4. During Sporisorium scitamineum infection, ShCOI1-4 is inducible in several cultivars, including smut-resistant lines, linking it to hormone- and pathogen-responsive defense signaling.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Hormone- and pathogen-responsive COI1-like gene whose expression is activated by ABA, SA, and smut infection and suppressed by cold and drought, suggesting involvement in sugarcane defense and abiotic stress signaling.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Transcriptionally up-regulated by ABA and SA and down-regulated by cold and PEG-induced drought; induction pattern under smut infection differs among cultivars, with stronger responses in some resistant lines.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: COI1-related jasmonate signaling module with cross-talk to ABA- and SA-mediated stress-response pathways in sugarcane.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: COI1 family F-box protein with F-box domain, Transp_inhibit (transport inhibitor response 1) domain, and AMN1 leucine-rich repeat (LRR) domain.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In ROC22 seedlings, ShCOI1-4 expression increases ~2.9-fold at 3–6 h after ABA treatment and ~1.6-fold at 12 h after SA, then returns to control levels; transcripts are reduced under cold (4 °C) and PEG-induced drought. Under S. scitamineum infection, ShCOI1-4 is induced in some cultivars (e.g., LC05-136 at 7 dpi, GT02-467 at 3 dpi, and in resistant YZ03-258 and YT96-86) but is decreased or stable in others.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salicylic acid (5 mM), abscisic acid (100 μM), cold (4 °C), drought simulated by 25% PEG 8000, and infection with the smut fungus Sporisorium scitamineum.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Hormone-responsive and stress-responsive regulation of COI1 genes in sugarcane, focusing on roles in biotic (smut disease) and abiotic (cold, drought) stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought and cold) and biotic stress response (smut disease caused by Sporisorium scitamineum).
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under ABA treatment, ShCOI1-4 transcripts increase ~2.86- and 2.99-fold at 3 h and 6 h, respectively; under SA treatment, expression peaks at 12 h at ~1.56-fold above control; cold and PEG treatments reduce transcript levels below control.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Sugarcane Saccharum spp. hybrid cultivar ROC22 plantlets for hormone and abiotic stress treatments; stems/buds of multiple cultivars (YZ03-258, LC05-136, YT96-86, GT02-467, FN40, YZ03-103) for smut infection assays.
Plant_Genes.Variety: ROC22 (prevalent sugarcane cultivar in mainland China); smut-resistant cultivars YZ03-258, LC05-136, YT96-86; smut-susceptible cultivars GT02-467, FN40, YZ03-103.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Homology-based cloning of COI1 homologs from ROC22 buds using RT-PCR and sequencing; RT-qPCR to quantify transcript levels under SA, ABA, cold, PEG-induced drought, and smut infection across multiple cultivars; statistical analysis using 2^-ΔΔCT and Duncan tests.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR-based expression analysis of cloned ShCOI1-4 under hormone (ABA, SA), abiotic (cold, PEG), and smut infection treatments.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a candidate COI1 homolog based on S. spontaneum SsCOI1 sequences, then cloned from ROC22 bud cDNA by homology-based RT-PCR and sequenced.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Because ShCOI1-4 is transcriptionally responsive to ABA, SA, drought, cold, and smut infection and shows stronger induction in some resistant cultivars, it represents a candidate gene/marker for molecular breeding to improve sugarcane resistance to smut and tolerance to abiotic stresses.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: ShCOI1-5 is a ROC22-derived homolog of SsCOI1-1b with 99.70% amino acid identity and shares conserved F-box, Transp_inhibit, and AMN1 (LRR) domains with other COI1 proteins. It retains only a subset of the 16 JA-Ile/JAZ-binding residues described in maize COI1 proteins, indicating potential functional diversification.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. ShCOI1-5 was cloned from sugarcane cultivar ROC22 as a homolog of SsCOI1-1b.
2. The encoded protein contains conserved F-box, Transp_inhibit, and AMN1 (LRR) domains typical of COI1 F-box proteins.
3. ShCOI1-5 is strongly induced by SA and PEG-induced drought, slightly affected or repressed by ABA, and largely unresponsive to cold.
4. During smut infection, ShCOI1-5 is significantly induced in all tested smut-resistant cultivars and in most susceptible cultivars, supporting a role in pathogen-responsive signaling.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Stress-responsive COI1-like gene whose expression is markedly activated by SA and drought and induced during smut infection, implicating it in hormone-mediated defense and abiotic stress responses in sugarcane.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Transcriptionally up-regulated by SA and PEG-induced drought, down-regulated by ABA at later time points, and not significantly changed by cold; broadly induced during smut infection in both resistant and susceptible cultivars except FN40.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: COI1-related jasmonate signaling component participating in cross-talk with SA- and ABA-mediated stress and defense pathways.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: COI1 family F-box protein with F-box domain, Transp_inhibit (transport inhibitor response 1) domain, and AMN1 leucine-rich repeat (LRR) domain.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In ROC22, ShCOI1-5 transcripts are strongly induced by SA with a single peak at 12 h (~2.17-fold above control). Under ABA, expression is unchanged at 0–6 h and reduced at 12 h. Under PEG-induced drought, transcripts increase ~2.77-fold at 0.5 h and ~1.66-fold at 3–6 h, while cold treatment does not significantly alter expression. During S. scitamineum infection, ShCOI1-5 is significantly up-regulated at 7 dpi in all three smut-resistant cultivars and in susceptible GT02-467 and YZ03-103, but down-regulated in susceptible FN40.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salicylic acid (5 mM), abscisic acid (100 μM), cold (4 °C), drought simulated by 25% PEG 8000, and Sporisorium scitamineum infection.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: COI1 gene family member involved in hormone (SA, ABA) responses and in biotic (smut) and abiotic (drought, cold) stress responses in sugarcane.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought and cold) and biotic stress response (smut disease caused by Sporisorium scitamineum).
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under SA treatment, ShCOI1-5 expression peaks at 12 h at ~2.17-fold of control. Under PEG-induced drought, transcripts reach ~2.77-fold at 0.5 h and ~1.66-fold at 3 h and 6 h. Under ABA, expression is reduced at 12 h relative to control; cold treatment shows no significant change.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Sugarcane ROC22 plantlets for hormone and abiotic stress treatments; buds of multiple cultivars (YZ03-258, LC05-136, YT96-86, GT02-467, FN40, YZ03-103) challenged with S. scitamineum for expression profiling.
Plant_Genes.Variety: ROC22; smut-resistant cultivars YZ03-258, LC05-136, YT96-86; smut-susceptible cultivars GT02-467, FN40, YZ03-103.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Homology-based cloning and sequencing of ShCOI1-5 from ROC22 buds; RT-qPCR to measure transcript levels under SA, ABA, cold, PEG-induced drought, and smut infection across cultivars; data analyzed via 2^-ΔΔCT and Duncan tests.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR analysis of ShCOI1-5 expression under hormone (SA, ABA), abiotic (cold, PEG), and smut infection treatments.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a COI1 candidate by homology to SsCOI1-1b and cloned from ROC22 bud cDNA using RT-PCR and sequence verification.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Because ShCOI1-5 is robustly induced by SA and drought and strongly up-regulated during smut infection in resistant cultivars, it is a promising candidate gene or expression marker for breeding sugarcane with enhanced resistance to smut and improved tolerance to drought stress.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: ShCOI1-6 is a ROC22-derived homolog of SsCOI1-3b with 99.2% amino acid identity and possesses conserved F-box, Transp_inhibit, and AMN1 (LRR) domains. It shares only part of the 16 JA-Ile/JAZ-binding residues characterized in maize COI1 proteins, indicating potential functional divergence among monocot COI1 proteins.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. ShCOI1-6 was cloned from sugarcane cultivar ROC22 as a homolog of SsCOI1-3b.
2. The encoded protein includes conserved F-box, Transp_inhibit, and AMN1 (LRR) domains typical of COI1 F-box proteins.
3. ShCOI1-6 expression is induced by SA, ABA, and PEG-induced drought but remains stable under cold stress.
4. During smut infection, ShCOI1-6 expression is increased in some resistant cultivars but decreased or unchanged in most susceptible cultivars, supporting its involvement in defense-related signaling.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: COI1-like gene whose transcripts are activated by SA, ABA, and drought and show cultivar-dependent induction during smut infection, suggesting a role in hormone- and stress-related defense responses in sugarcane.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Transcriptionally induced by SA (early), by ABA from 3–12 h, and by PEG-induced drought (notably at 24 h), while remaining unchanged under cold; during smut infection, induction is evident in some resistant cultivars but not in most susceptible ones.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: COI1-related jasmonate signaling component participating in ABA- and SA-associated stress and immune pathways in sugarcane.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: COI1 family F-box protein with F-box domain, Transp_inhibit (transport inhibitor response 1) domain, and AMN1 leucine-rich repeat (LRR) domain.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In ROC22 seedlings, ShCOI1-6 expression is significantly induced by SA at 3 h (~1.86-fold of control), by ABA from 3–12 h with a peak at 12 h, and by PEG-induced drought, reaching ~2.14-fold of control at 24 h; cold treatment does not alter expression. During S. scitamineum infection, ShCOI1-6 is significantly up-regulated in resistant cultivars YZ03-258 and YT96-86, whereas it is decreased or remains stable in the other four cultivars.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salicylic acid (5 mM), abscisic acid (100 μM), cold (4 °C), drought simulated by 25% PEG 8000, and Sporisorium scitamineum infection.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Functional characterization of a COI1 family member in hormone signaling (SA, ABA) and in biotic (smut) and abiotic (drought, cold) stress responses in sugarcane.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (drought and cold) and biotic stress response (smut disease caused by Sporisorium scitamineum).
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: SA treatment increases ShCOI1-6 transcripts to ~1.86-fold of control at 3 h. Under ABA, expression rises from 3–12 h, peaking at 12 h above control levels. PEG-induced drought elevates expression, with a significant ~2.14-fold increase at 24 h. Cold treatment produces no significant change.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: ROC22 sugarcane plantlets for hormone and abiotic stress treatments; buds of resistant cultivars YZ03-258, LC05-136, YT96-86 and susceptible cultivars GT02-467, FN40, YZ03-103 for smut infection assays.
Plant_Genes.Variety: ROC22; smut-resistant cultivars YZ03-258, LC05-136, YT96-86; smut-susceptible cultivars GT02-467, FN40, YZ03-103.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Homology-based cloning and sequencing of ShCOI1-6 from ROC22 buds; RT-qPCR to quantify expression under SA, ABA, cold, PEG-induced drought, and smut infection across cultivars; analysis by 2^-ΔΔCT and Duncan tests.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression profiling of ShCOI1-6 under hormone (SA, ABA), abiotic (cold, PEG), and smut infection treatments.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Selected as a COI1 candidate based on homology to SsCOI1-3b and cloned from ROC22 bud cDNA using RT-PCR followed by sequencing.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Because ShCOI1-6 is induced by SA, ABA, drought, and smut infection, particularly in resistant cultivars, it is a potential target for molecular breeding or marker-assisted selection to enhance smut resistance and tolerance to abiotic stresses in sugarcane.
